title: calocephalus dittrichii
intro: Calocephalus dittrichii, also known as the Silver Chai or Dillies Bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Calocephalus dittrichii include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water, as well as dried and powdered forms of the plant used in capsules or topical creams.
